mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE), a managed Kubernetes service on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. OKE simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters, allowing you to focus on your applications rather than the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Oracle Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Oracle Cloud CLI, which is necessary for managing resources in Oracle Cloud.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the OCI CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.0 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual compartment and VCN IDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.0 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region>

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual cluster ID and region.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/oci-cloud-controller-manager.yaml

kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

```

> This command deploys the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.
> Replace <your_user_id> with your actual user ID to grant cluster admin permissions.
commands: kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

```

> This command deletes the cluster and waits for it to be fully terminated.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

oci bv volume delete --volume-id <your_volume_id> --force

```

> Replace <your_volume_id> with the ID of any orphaned volumes.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current state of the nodes.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.30.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of the nodes before proceeding.
> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that the nodes are in a 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the kubeconfig file is not correctly configured or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the kubeconfig file is correctly set up and points to the right cluster context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Deploy resources in the default namespace or specify the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this operation'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user does not have the necessary permissions to perform the requested operation.

**Fix:**
Create a cluster role binding to grant the user the required permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the VCN or subnets are not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Verify the VCN and subnet configurations in the OCI console.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features and improved integration with OCI services.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure that all custom resources are compatible with v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, indicating that your OKE cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.0 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'
kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and configured before proceeding.
